_doc: Full Nike-internal example config showing the modern auth-provider
broker pattern alongside legacy passthrough/static modes.
Pair with configs/example-auth-providers.json for the matching
providers config:
make load ZELOSMCP_CONFIG=configs/example-passthrough-zelosmcp.json \
ZELOSMCP_AUTH_PROVIDERS_FILE=configs/example-auth-providers.json
Three auth modes shown:
* github / githubcopilot — broker mode via 'auth.provider' ->
'github_oauth_app'. zelosMCP mints user-OAuth tokens via the
GUI device flow and injects them on outbound requests. The
backend's wrappers stay HIDDEN in tools/list until the user
completes the device flow in the Connections page.
* atlassian / artifactory — broker mode via 'auth.provider' ->
'nike_okta'. Same browser-Connect UX; tokens come from Nike's Okta
tenant. The 'membership_hint' field on the okta provider in
the providers config surfaces the authorized-group requirement
(e.g. Nike.uee.maria) on the GUI card.
* box — legacy passthrough mode (no provider). Forwards inbound
Authorization verbatim — Cursor's MCP OAuth client handles the
dance against box.com directly. Backend's wrappers are visible
by default (no gating).
For headless / CI deploys without browser access, switch a backend
to the 'static' provider (see ci_pat in example-auth-providers.json)
and reference it via 'auth.provider' here.
